text:
BOTTOM LINE — Rangebound, small-mid bid, metals and energy dumped. With all majors flat on the day and only IJH up +0.76%, the tape reads as a pause after a strong June with factor leadership still skewed to value and momentum despite near-term tech and commodities fatigue. Fear & Greed at 64.3/100 with VIX at 16.4 and CPI/PCE still hawkish says positioning is pro‑risk but not euphoric, while sharp 20d drawdowns in GLD (-10.43%), SLV (-20.98%), and USO (-21.45%) point to macro de‑risking in metals and energy rather than broad equity stress.

INDICES — VIX 16.4, below its 20d MA 18.1 and mid‑range in the 52‑week 13.5–31.1 band. Best to worst 1d: IJH +0.76%, then QQQ/DIA/SPY/RSP/IWM/IJR/SOXX/GLD/SLV all at +0.00% or below, with SOXX still +104.27% ytd and IJR +22.20% ytd versus SPY +9.31% ytd. STYLE: LARGE > SMALL by 0.00pp (small=+0.00% large=+0.00%), effectively neutral with prior weeks still favoring small caps via IJR’s +7.11% 20d vs SPY’s -1.55%. Most notable cross‑asset move is the sustained 20d crash in USO at -21.45%, alongside GLD -10.43% and SLV -20.98%, while UUP is +2.34% over 20d and TLT is +1.11%, a classic strong‑dollar, falling‑commodities setup.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.0x and QQQ 1.1x with flat prices points to low‑conviction, rangebound trade rather than strong accumulation or distribution. Index breadth shows IWM volume elevated at 1.2x vs IJR at 0.8x, suggesting attention on broader small caps but fading focus on micro‑caps. Sector breadth has XLRE at 1.7x, XLP and XLV at 1.0x, while XLY, XLK, and XLB sit 0.7–0.8x, indicating incremental interest in defensives and real estate and muted engagement in discretionary, tech, and materials.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +0.00%, XLI Industrials +0.00%, XLC Comms +0.00%, XLF Financials +0.00%, XLU Utilities +0.00%, XLY Discretionary +0.00%, XLRE Real Estate +0.00%, XLP Staples +0.00%, XLB Materials +0.00%, XLE Energy +0.00%, XLV Healthcare +0.00%. Top industries: DRAM +2.65%, GXC +0.72%, FDN +0.21%, FCG +0.11%; bottom: XHB -0.07%, XRT -0.23%, OIH -0.25%, UTES -2.10%. Money‑in is skewed toward DRAM/memory and China‑linked GXC, while money‑out is focused on utilities (UTES -2.10%) and retail via XRT, consistent with ongoing AI‑beneficiary strength and rate‑sensitive weakness. With SOXX up +104.27% ytd, DRAM +2.65% today, and KLAC showing a 2.4x vol spike, the rotation‑state label is: AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 0.85 with ATM_IV 10.4% and skew +5.4% indicates modest protection demand but no panic, consistent with VIX below its 20d MA. QQQ PCR 1.27 and skew +13.0% plus IWM PCR 1.53 with skew +8.6% show heavier downside hedging in growth and small caps, fitting a tech‑heavy, high‑beta leadership tape where investors are long but paying for puts. With SPY GEX_flip at 748.0 and QQQ at 729.0, and spot indices near recent highs, dealers are likely long gamma, dampening intraday volatility unless spot breaks below flip levels.

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar strength via UUP +2.34% over 20d alongside TLT +1.11% and IEF +0.42% contrasts sharply with GLD -10.43%, SLV -20.98%, and USO -21.45% over 20d, a transmission chain of stronger USD and easing yields crushing commodities. The combination of falling metals and energy with stable credit (HYG +0.18% 1d, LQD +0.14% 20d) points to macro re‑pricing of inflation and growth expectations rather than credit stress.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed: 64.3/100 — Greed, with top individual movers: DRAM +2.65% (semis), GXC +0.72% (China), UTES -2.10% (utilities), and 20d extremes showing SOXX +12.03% as best basket and SLV -20.98% as worst. Vol spike tickers: IJK 3.1x with +1.34% price in S&P Style ETFs (accumulation), IJT 2.8x flat (churn), KLAC 2.4x flat in The Fab 7 (positioning churn in AI hardware), MLM 2.2x flat in The Housing 7 (rate‑sensitive watch), CVX 2.2x flat in The Energy 7 (energy de‑risking), BSX 2.1x flat in The Medtech 7 (quiet accumulation). Biggest contextual mover is DRAM +2.65%, lining up with the ongoing AI buildout and semis’ +90%+ run since March highlighted in recent recaps. Watch list: IJK +1.34% on 3.1x volume for style rotation into mid‑caps; KLAC +0.00% on 2.4x volume for potential inflection in AI hardware leadership; UTES -2.10% with sector‑wide utilities weakness signaling stress in yield‑sensitive defensives.
